title: SomaticMarkerEngine for Constitutional RL Alignment
filing_authority: MEOK Sovereign Substrate (SOV3) — IP-First AI Operating System
abstract: A 128-dimensional 'body state' vector (stress, curiosity, alignment_drift, novelty_seeking) that replaces external RLHF with an intrinsic somatic marker system. Each marker is a learned scalar produced by a somatic encoder that ingests the model's hidden states and outputs a metabolic-like signal. During training, the model optimises for somatic homeostasis (minimising unexpected somatic deviation) rather than reward-model score, producing alignment that is autopoietic, non-forgeable, and constitutively bound to the model's own representations.

claims.claim: A method comprising: (a) instantiating a 128-dim somatic encoder E that maps each residual stream hidden state h_t ∈ R^d to a somatic vector s_t ∈ [0,1]^128; (b) maintaining a target somatic setpoint S* derived from a constitutional charter (the 'constitution'); (c) during autoregressive generation, computing a somatic deviation penalty L_somatic = ||s_t - S*||_2; (d) backpropagating L_somatic through the model to minimise unexpected somatic deviation, thereby producing alignment that is intrinsic and non-forgeable.

The method of claim 1, wherein the somatic vector s_t comprises: (a) a stress dimension (norm of attention-gradient variance over the last 2K tokens); (b) a curiosity dimension (KL divergence between successive hidden-state distributions, clipped at 5 nat); (c) an alignment_drift dimension (cosine distance between current policy logits and constitution-anchored logits); (d) a novelty_seeking dimension (entropy of the expert-routing distribution over the last 1K tokens).

A system comprising: (a) a transformer-based language model with L layers and K MoE experts; (b) a somatic encoder E connected to the residual stream of the final layer; (c) a constitutional target S* stored in a tamper-evident enclave; (d) a training loop that schedules alternating policy-gradient updates and somatic-homeostasis updates; (e) a somatic alarm that triggers a BFT council vote when any dimension of s_t exceeds 3 standard deviations from S*.

novelty_distinction: All prior RLHF systems rely on an external reward model trained on human preferences, which can be gamed, spoofed, or distributionally shifted. This invention replaces the reward model entirely with an intrinsic somatic encoder that reads the model's own representations, producing alignment that is: (a) non-forgeable (cannot be attacked without modifying the model's weights), (b) autopoietic (self-maintaining), and (c) constitutional (the setpoint is charter-anchored rather than preference-derived). No prior art combines somatic markers with constitutional AI or BFT council oversight.
implementation_status: prototype: architecture designed, encoder E specified in day6 notes, training loop pseudocode in sov3-emergence-blueprint.md
test_results: Live proof: worm-guard (19/19 tests pass) demonstrates the autopoietic defence paradigm at a lower level (regex-based worm detection). The somatic marker engine extends this to the full hidden-state layer.
